wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the PDF Preflight Module overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a production-minded Java-based PDF preflight engine for validating page dimensions and orientation consistency in large PDF files (up to 1GB). Document the core value proposition, target audience, and key differentiators from similar tools like callas pdfToolbox. Include the complete feature set with detailed explanations of dimension validation, orientation validation, large file support, configurable tolerance, flexible box selection, dual report output, optional rasterization, and modular architecture. Provide architectural overview showing the component relationships and design decisions. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples demonstrating the tool's capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the PDF Preflight Module. Include detailed installation instructions covering prerequisites (Java 11+, Maven/Gradle, optional MuPDF), dependency installation for different platforms (macOS, Ubuntu/Debian), and multiple build options (build script, Maven, Gradle). Document the complete build process and explain the resulting executable fat JAR structure. Provide step-by-step first-time usage examples starting with basic validation commands, progressing to more advanced configurations. Include troubleshooting for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make the content accessible to users with varying technical backgrounds while providing sufficient detail for successful implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Command-Line Interface Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed command-line interface documentation for the PDF Preflight Module. Document all command-line options including required arguments (--input) and optional parameters (--output-json, --output-text, --use-mediabox, --tolerance, --rasterize, --raster-dpi, --mutool-path, --raster-pages, --help). Provide comprehensive usage examples for each option combination, explaining parameter types, default values, and expected behaviors. Document the complete exit code system (0=pass, 1=fail, 2=error) with detailed explanations of when each code is returned. Include practical command patterns for common use cases, troubleshooting command-line issues, and integration examples for CI/CD pipelines. Present the information in a clear, searchable format with syntax highlighting and parameter descriptions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration management documentation for the PdfPreflightConfig builder pattern. Explain the fluent configuration API, available configuration options including tolerance settings, box selection preferences, and rasterization parameters. Document the builder pattern implementation with method chaining examples and demonstrate how to programmatically configure the preflight engine. Include best practices for configuration management, default values, and recommended settings for different use cases. Provide code examples showing both builder pattern usage and configuration object inspection. Address configuration validation, error handling, and integration with the service layer. Make the content accessible to developers implementing programmatic integrations while providing sufficient technical depth for advanced customization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Validation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core validation engine focusing on PdfDimensionChecker implementation. Explain the combined dimension and orientation validation algorithm, including the single-pass processing approach for efficiency. Document the floating-point comparison logic with tolerance settings, the orientation detection rules (width >= height = landscape, height > width = portrait), and the box selection strategy (CropBox preference with MediaBox fallback). Include detailed explanations of the validation decision criteria, performance characteristics, and memory management strategies. Provide code examples demonstrating the validation logic, edge case handling, and integration with the broader preflight system. Address common validation scenarios, error conditions, and optimization techniques for large PDF processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Structures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the immutable object design used throughout the preflight engine. Document PdfPreflightResult as the main result container, PdfPageInfo for immutable page dimension storage, and PageMismatch for detailed mismatch reporting. Explain the thread-safe immutable design choices, constructor parameters, getter methods, and data validation rules. Include detailed field descriptions, data types, and business rule enforcement. Provide examples of object creation, manipulation, and serialization. Document the relationships between these models and their role in the overall validation workflow. Address data flow patterns, performance benefits of immutability, and integration with report generation systems. Include JSON representation examples for the machine-readable output format.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the report generation system architecture and implementation. Document the PdfReportWriter interface design and the strategy pattern implementation for multiple output formats. Explain the JSON report format specification with complete schema documentation, field definitions, and example outputs. Detail the text report formatting and structure, including human-readable presentation and summary statistics. Document the report generation workflow, data transformation processes, and output file management. Include examples of report customization, extension possibilities, and integration with external systems. Address report performance considerations, memory usage during generation, and best practices for report consumption in automated workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Optional Rasterization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the optional rasterization functionality using MuPDF integration. Explain the rasterization workflow, configuration options including DPI settings and page selection, and the isolation from core validation logic. Document MuPDF installation requirements, path configuration, and platform-specific considerations. Include detailed examples of rasterization execution, output format specifications, and quality settings. Address the optional nature of rasterization, performance implications, and integration with the validation workflow. Provide troubleshooting guidance for MuPDF-related issues, alternative rasterization approaches, and best practices for visual verification workflows. Document the relationship between validation failures and rasterization triggers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the PdfPreflightService orchestration layer. Describe the service layer's role as the central coordinator between CLI, validation logic, report generation, and optional rasterization. Document the service workflow, error handling strategies, and integration patterns with other components. Explain the business logic implementation, validation orchestration, and result aggregation processes. Include detailed method documentation, parameter handling, and return value specifications. Address threading considerations, resource management, and performance optimization techniques. Document the service's extensibility points, integration patterns for programmatic usage, and best practices for custom implementations. Provide examples of service usage in different contexts and integration with external systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build and deployment documentation for the PDF Preflight Module. Document the dual build system support (Maven and Gradle) with detailed configuration explanations and dependency management. Explain the fat JAR creation process, shade plugin usage, and executable JAR structure. Include build optimization techniques, dependency version management, and compatibility considerations. Document deployment strategies, distribution methods, and packaging best practices. Address continuous integration setup, automated testing integration, and release management processes. Provide troubleshooting guidance for build issues, dependency conflicts, and platform-specific deployment challenges. Include examples of custom build configurations and extension points for specialized deployment requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed testing strategy documentation for the PDF Preflight Module. Document the unit testing approach using JUnit 5, test coverage scenarios including empty PDFs, single pages, multiple page validations, and edge cases. Explain the integration testing methodology, test data management, and the role of test PDFs in validation workflows. Include examples of test implementation patterns, mocking strategies, and assertion techniques. Document the testing framework setup, test execution procedures, and continuous integration testing practices. Address test performance considerations, memory usage during testing, and optimization techniques for large PDF testing. Provide guidance for extending test coverage, adding new test scenarios, and maintaining test quality in the face of evolving requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation for all public interfaces and classes in the PDF Preflight Module. Document the PreflightCli entry point with complete method signatures, parameters, and usage examples. Provide detailed API documentation for PdfPreflightService including all public methods, parameters, return values, and exception handling. Document the PdfPreflightConfig builder pattern with fluent API methods and configuration options. Include complete class documentation for all model classes (PdfPreflightResult, PdfPageInfo, PageMismatch) with field descriptions and usage patterns. Document the report writer interfaces and implementations with method specifications and extension points. Provide the PdfRasterizer API documentation with configuration options and integration examples. Include code examples for each major API component, integration patterns, and best practices for programmatic usage. Address API versioning, backward compatibility considerations, and migration guidance for future updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues and their solutions for the PDF Preflight Module. Document memory-related problems including OutOfMemoryError mitigation strategies for large PDFs, heap size recommendations, and disk space requirements. Address MuPDF integration issues including installation problems, path configuration, and platform-specific considerations. Include detailed guidance for handling corrupted or encrypted PDFs, missing file scenarios, and runtime errors. Document performance optimization techniques, processing time improvements, and resource usage monitoring. Provide diagnostic procedures for identifying validation failures, interpreting report outputs, and debugging custom configurations. Include frequently asked questions about tool limitations, supported PDF features, and integration scenarios. Address environment setup issues, dependency conflicts, and upgrade procedures with step-by-step resolution guides.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive contributing guidelines for the PDF Preflight Module development community. Document the development environment setup, code style conventions, and contribution workflow. Explain the project structure, coding standards, and architectural principles that contributors should follow. Include detailed instructions for setting up the development environment, running tests, and building the project. Document the pull request process, code review expectations, and quality standards. Provide guidance for adding new validation checks, extending report formats, and integrating additional PDF features. Include examples of good contributions, common pitfalls to avoid, and best practices for maintaining code quality. Address testing requirements, documentation standards, and release procedures. Provide contact information and community resources for contributors seeking assistance or collaboration opportunities.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# PreFlighter 2.0: Comprehensive PDF Preflight Validation Engine

## Project Introduction

PreFlighter 2.0 is a production-minded Java-based PDF preflight engine designed to validate page dimensions and orientation consistency in large PDF files (up to 1GB). The tool serves as a lightweight alternative to enterprise solutions like callas pdfToolbox, focusing specifically on dimension and orientation validation with optional rasterization capabilities.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Dimension Validation**: Ensure all pages have consistent width and height measurements
- **Orientation Validation**: Verify all pages maintain the same orientation (portrait/landscape)
- **Large File Support**: Handle PDFs up to 1GB+ using memory-efficient page-by-page processing
- **CI/CD Integration**: Provide clear exit codes (0, 1, 2) for seamless automation
- **Extensible Architecture**: Modular design allowing easy addition of new validation checks

### Target Audience

- **Print Production Teams**: Quality assurance for print-ready PDFs
- **Digital Asset Managers**: Maintaining consistency across large document collections
- **Automation Engineers**: Integrating PDF validation into CI/CD pipelines
- **Content Publishers**: Ensuring uniform page specifications across publications

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a clean, modular architecture with clear separation of concerns and well-defined responsibilities.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[PreflightCli] --> B[PdfPreflightService]
    B --> C[PdfDimensionChecker]
    B --> D[JsonReportWriter]
    B --> E[TextReportWriter]
    B --> F[PdfRasterizer]
    C --> G[PdfPageInfo]
    C --> H[PageMismatch]
    D --> I[PdfPreflightResult]
    E --> I
    F --> J[MuPDF CLI]
    
    K[Configuration] --> B
    L[Models] --> C
    M[Reports] --> D
    M --> E
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of six primary modules:

1. **CLI Layer**: Command-line interface for user interaction
2. **Service Layer**: Orchestration and business logic coordination
3. **Checker Layer**: Core validation algorithms
4. **Model Layer**: Immutable data structures
5. **Report Layer**: Multiple output format generation
6. **Rasterizer Layer**: Optional page rendering functionality

### Design Patterns

- **Builder Pattern**: `PdfPreflightConfig.Builder` for flexible configuration management
- **Strategy Pattern**: `PdfReportWriter` interface enabling multiple output formats
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Centralized orchestration in `PdfPreflightService`
- **Immutable Objects**: All model classes are immutable for thread safety

### Data Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CLI as PreflightCli
    participant Service as PdfPreflightService
    participant Checker as PdfDimensionChecker
    participant Reports as Report Writers
    participant Raster as PdfRasterizer
    
    CLI->>Service: execute(config)
    Service->>Checker: checkDimensions(document, config)
    Checker-->>Service: List<PageMismatch>
    Service->>Reports: writeReports(result)
    alt Rasterization Enabled
        Service->>Raster: rasterizeFailedPages()
        Raster-->>Service: rasterization status
    end
    Service-->>CLI: PdfPreflightResult
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application provides two primary entry points:

- **Command-Line Interface**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/PreflightCli.java` - Handles argument parsing and execution flow
- **Service Orchestrator**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/service/PdfPreflightService.java` - Core business logic coordination

### Core Modules

#### Configuration Management
- **PdfPreflightConfig**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/config/PdfPreflightConfig.java` - Builder-pattern configuration with 143 lines of code
- **Design Pattern**: Implements fluent builder for type-safe configuration construction

#### Validation Logic
- **PdfDimensionChecker**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/checker/PdfDimensionChecker.java` - Single-pass validation algorithm with 139 lines
- **Algorithm**: Combines dimension and orientation checks in one iteration for optimal performance

#### Data Models
- **PdfPageInfo**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/model/PdfPageInfo.java` - Immutable page information container
- **PageMismatch**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/model/PageMismatch.java` - Detailed mismatch reporting
- **PdfPreflightResult**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/model/PdfPreflightResult.java` - Comprehensive result aggregation

#### Reporting System
- **PdfReportWriter Interface**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/report/PdfReportWriter.java` - Strategy pattern for report generation
- **JsonReportWriter**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/report/JsonReportWriter.java` - Structured JSON output with Jackson
- **TextReportWriter**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/report/TextReportWriter.java` - Human-readable formatted reports

#### Optional Rasterization
- **PdfRasterizer**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/rasterizer/PdfRasterizer.java` - MuPDF CLI integration for page rendering
- **Isolation**: Completely separate from core validation logic

### Build Configuration

The project supports multiple build systems:

- **Maven**: `pom.xml` with shade plugin for executable JAR creation
- **Gradle**: `build.gradle` with application plugin and fat JAR configuration
- **Build Script**: `build.sh` for automated compilation and packaging

### External Dependencies

Key dependencies managed through Maven/Gradle:

- **Apache PDFBox 2.0.30**: Core PDF parsing and manipulation
- **Jackson 2.15.3**: JSON serialization/deserialization
- **SLF4J 2.0.9**: Logging abstraction and implementation
- **JUnit 5.10.1**: Testing framework

### Integration Points

- **MuPDF CLI**: Optional rasterization using `mutool` command-line utility
- **System Environment**: Direct integration with operating system for file I/O and process execution
- **Logging System**: SLF4J integration for configurable logging levels

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class PreflightCli {
        +main(String[])
        +parseArgs(String[])
        +printSummary(PdfPreflightResult)
    }
    
    class PdfPreflightService {
        -PdfDimensionChecker dimensionChecker
        -JsonReportWriter jsonReportWriter
        -TextReportWriter textReportWriter
        +execute(PdfPreflightConfig)
        -writeReports(PdfPreflightResult, PdfPreflightConfig)
    }
    
    class PdfDimensionChecker {
        +checkDimensions(PDDocument, PdfPreflightConfig)
        -getPageBox(PDPage, boolean)
        -calculateOrientation(float, float)
    }
    
    class PdfPreflightConfig {
        <<builder>>
        +Builder builder(String)
        +build()
    }
    
    PreflightCli --> PdfPreflightService
    PdfPreflightService --> PdfDimensionChecker
    PdfPreflightService --> PdfPreflightConfig
    PdfPreflightService --> JsonReportWriter
    PdfPreflightService --> TextReportWriter
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

1. **Dimension Consistency Validation**: Compares page widths and heights against reference page
2. **Orientation Standardization**: Ensures consistent portrait/landscape orientation across all pages
3. **Large File Processing**: Memory-efficient handling of PDFs up to 1GB using temp-file-only mode
4. **Configurable Tolerance**: Adjustable floating-point comparison threshold (default: 0.01 points)
5. **Dual Report Generation**: Simultaneous JSON (machine-readable) and text (human-readable) output
6. **Optional Rasterization**: Visual verification of failed pages using MuPDF CLI
7. **Comprehensive Error Handling**: Graceful handling of corrupted, encrypted, or missing PDFs

### Implementation Highlights

#### Memory-Efficient Processing
- **Temp-File-Only Mode**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/service/PdfPreflightService.java` - Uses PDFBox's `MemoryUsageSetting.setupTempFileOnly()` for large file support
- **Streaming Architecture**: Page-by-page processing prevents memory overflow with large documents

#### Modular Validation System
- **Single-Pass Algorithm**: `src/main/java/com/preflight/checker/PdfDimensionChecker.java` - Combines dimension and orientation checks for optimal performance
- **Extensible Design**: Easy integration of additional validation checks following established patterns

#### Robust Error Handling
- **Exit Code System**: 0 (pass), 1 (fail), 2 (error) for seamless CI/CD integration
- **Descriptive Messages**: Comprehensive error reporting for troubleshooting

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> InputValidation
    InputValidation --> FileLoading: Valid Input
    InputValidation --> ErrorExit: Invalid Input
    FileLoading --> DimensionCheck: File Loaded
    FileLoading --> ErrorExit: Load Failed
    DimensionCheck --> OrientationCheck: Dimensions OK
    DimensionCheck --> MismatchFound: Dimensions Mismatch
    OrientationCheck --> ReportGeneration: All Checks Passed
    OrientationCheck --> MismatchFound: Orientation Mismatch
    MismatchFound --> ReportGeneration: Generate Reports
    ReportGeneration --> Rasterization: Rasterization Enabled
    ReportGeneration --> SuccessExit: No Mismatches
    Rasterization --> SuccessExit: Rasterization Complete
    ErrorExit --> [*]
    SuccessExit --> [*]
```

### Performance Characteristics

- **Memory Usage**: < 256MB heap for 500MB PDF with 1000 pages
- **Processing Time**: Typically 2-5 seconds for large documents (dependent on disk I/O)
- **Scalability**: Designed for linear scaling with document size through streaming architecture

### Quality Assurance

- **Comprehensive Testing**: 17 test scenarios covering edge cases and error conditions
- **Integration Tests**: End-to-end validation of complete workflow
- **Unit Tests**: Isolated testing of individual components
- **Documentation**: Complete API documentation and usage examples

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](pdf-preflight/README.md)
- [PreflightCli.java](pdf-preflight/src/main/java/com/preflight/PreflightCli.java)
- [PdfPreflightService.java](pdf-preflight/src/main/java/com/preflight/service/PdfPreflightService.java)
- [PdfDimensionChecker.java](pdf-preflight/src/main/java/com/preflight/checker/PdfDimensionChecker.java)
- [PdfPreflightConfig.java](pdf-preflight/src/main/java/com/preflight/config/PdfPreflightConfig.java)
- [PdfPageInfo.java](pdf-preflight/src/main/java/com/preflight/model/PdfPageInfo.java)
- [JsonReportWriter.java](pdf-preflight/src/main/java/com/preflight/report/JsonReportWriter.java)
- [PdfRasterizer.java](pdf-preflight/src/main/java/com/preflight/rasterizer/PdfRasterizer.java)
- [build.gradle](pdf-preflight/build.gradle)
- [pom.xml](pdf-preflight/pom.xml)
- [PdfPreflightServiceTest.java](pdf-preflight/src/test/java/com/preflight/PdfPreflightServiceTest.java)
- [IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md](pdf-preflight/IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md)

</blog>
